message:
Implement recursive import compiler

Agent 1 completed:
- Created import_scanner.zig (139 lines)
- Scans Python files for import/from statements
- Builds dependency graph recursively
- Auto-compiles all imported modules

Changes to compile.zig:
- Phase 2.3: Import dependency scanning
- Compiles each module to build/lib.{platform}/
- Uses cache to skip up-to-date modules

How it works:
1. Parse main file for imports
2. Recursively scan each import for its imports
3. Build full dependency graph
4. Compile each .py → .so in build/

Example:
  import test_mymodule
  → Scans test_mymodule.py
  → Compiles to build/lib.../test_mymodule.cpython-312-darwin.so

Status: Import scanning works ✅
Next: Fix module exports (functions not exported yet)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Test recursive import compilation
+import test_mymodule
+
+result = test_mymodule.greet("World")
+print(result)
+
+num = test_mymodule.add(5, 3)
+print(num)
+
+print("Version:", test_mymodule.VERSION)
